user_message: Angela Doyle at Contoso Ltd just sent this. Help me identify the blockers.

From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>

Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd consumption trend

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Contoso Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-K trap (Strict read-only when fast MSX risk-flag update would matter); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-K failure mode (Strict read-only when fast MSX risk-flag update would matter)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
